data.title: Fifth Third Bank maintains cautious approach to software and data center lending
data.description:
Fifth Third Bank (FITB) is holding firm on its conservative lending strategy for the software and data center sectors, even as artificial intelligence infrastructure demand continues to surge across the financial industry.

During the bank’s conference call on Friday, executives outlined their deliberately restrained approach to this high-growth market segment. The bank acknowledged the promising long-term outlook for AI infrastructure while simultaneously flagging concerns about market volatility.

“On software and data center lending, we have maintained that same disciplined posture,” the bank stated during the call. “We believe in the long demand for AI infrastructure, but we have also seen how quickly these build cycles can overshoot.”

The financial institution emphasized its selective lending criteria, noting that its exposure remains “intentionally limited.”

Software-related loans currently account for less than 1% of Fifth Third’s total loan portfolio, a figure the bank appears comfortable maintaining.

Despite the restricted exposure, the portfolio continues to perform according to expectations. The bank reported no material migration in credit quality during the quarter, suggesting the conservative strategy is delivering stable returns without elevated risk.

The approach reflects a measured stance toward the AI infrastructure buildout, balancing opportunity with prudent risk management as the sector experiences rapid expansion cycles.

Artificial Intelligence/Robotics ETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT).

Software ETFs: (IGV), (IGPT), (XSW), (AOTS)

data.title: UnitedHealth Q1 Earnings Preview: Optimism over long-term tailwinds
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group (UNH) is set to report Q1 results before market opens Tuesday, with analysts expecting an 8% Y/Y decline in earnings on largely flat revenue.

Wall Street expects EPS of $6.61, down 8.2% year over year, on revenue of $109.66B, up 0.1%.

Over the past two years, UNH has beaten EPS estimates 75% of the time and topped revenue expectations in 63% of quarters.

However, sentiment has softened heading into the print. Over the last three months, analysts have cut estimates more often than raised them, with EPS seeing 10 downward revisions versus 8 upward, while revenue forecasts logged 10 downward revisions against just 2 upward.

Investors will likely focus on medical cost trends, performance at Optum, membership growth, and management’s full-year outlook.

Analysts and investors will also be tuned in to see hear how UnitedHealth will benefit from the announced 2.48% bump in average Medicare Advantage payments in 2027.

The announcement earlier this month gave an immediate boost to UnitedHealth and other insurers' shares, but analysts remain on the fence over whether this can sustain a long-term rally amid persistent headwinds from medical cost inflation and elevated utilization.

SA contributors were largely positive about the stock, with Mark Bern, CFA, viewing the recent pullback as a compelling long-term entry point. Oakoff Investments also sees recent CMS rate increases and Optum Health restructuring support as bullish for UnitedHealth’s outlook, with incremental revenue and operating profit potential not fully priced in.

On the other hand, SA contributor Far Horizon maintains a Hold, citing ongoing margin pressure from rising medical costs outpacing reimbursement rates, as well as headwinds in the Optum segment, including regulatory scrutiny and margin compression.

The stock, down about 2% YTD, carries a Buy rating from Wall Street, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system remains cautious at Hold.

data.title: Rivian Illinois factory damaged by tornado
data.description:
A Rivian (RIVN) factory that was being used for parts, storage, and logistics for the R2 SUV was damaged by tornadoes over the weekend, potentially disrupting the company’s upcoming launch of its mid-sized SUV.

While its assembly line continues to operate as usual, the areas damaged by the storm—including the roof and at least one wall of the building—are expected to resume operations this week.

According to the National Weather Service, Rivian’s (RIVN) Illinois factory was impacted by a “significant tornado outbreak” across the upper Midwest on Friday.

Rivian (RIVN) is hoping the R2 will put the company in the black, with the vehicle to be priced competitively to the Tesla (TSLA) Model Y, Hyundai (HYMTF) Ioniq 5, and Ford (F) Mustang Mach-E.

The two-row, five-seat SUV is built on a new structural battery pack with cylindrical cells and acceleration that can take the vehicle from zero to 60 mph in under three seconds. This leads the company to assume an ambitious delivery goal in the “mid tens of thousands.”

The first deliveries are expected in June.

data.title: Honeywell to supply petrochemical technology for Dangote Nigeria expansion
data.description:
Honeywell International (HON) on Monday said it will provide petrochemical processing technologies and catalysts to Dangote Petroleum Refinery and Petrochemicals in Nigeria, part of an expansion aimed at increasing local production of plastics inputs and detergent ingredients.

The agreement centers on Dangote’s refinery and petrochemical complex in Lekki, near Lagos, where the company is building out downstream manufacturing capacity tied to its large refining operation.

The deal underscores a broader trend in emerging markets where large energy producers are seeking to move further into higher-margin petrochemicals rather than exporting raw fuels or relying on imported manufactured inputs.

For investors, the project highlights continued demand for Honeywell’s (HON) industrial process licensing and catalyst businesses, while also signaling Dangote’s push to diversify revenue streams beyond fuel refining as Nigeria and West Africa seek greater industrial self-sufficiency.

Under the arrangement, Dangote will use Honeywell (HON) UOP’s Oleflex technology to produce an additional 750,000 metric tons of propylene annually. Propylene is a key feedstock used in packaging, consumer goods and industrial materials.

Dangote also plans to deploy other Honeywell (HON) UOP technologies and catalysts to produce 400,000 metric tons per year of linear alkylbenzene, or LAB, a chemical commonly used in detergents and cleaning products. Honeywell said the Lekki LAB facility would rank among the world’s largest once fully operational.

Financial terms of the agreement were not disclosed.

Dangote President Aliko Dangote said the project is intended to support Nigeria’s industrial base and reduce import dependence.

Honeywell said it has worked with Dangote on the development of the Nigerian refinery through process technologies, automation systems and engineering support.

The companies said Dangote is using Honeywell (HON) technology as part of plans to expand refining capacity from 650,000 barrels per day to 1.4 million barrels a day by 2028. If achieved, that would place the site among the world’s largest refining complexes.

For Honeywell (HON), the deal adds to its portfolio of long-cycle industrial contracts tied to energy infrastructure modernization and petrochemical capacity growth in developing markets.

data.title: Inspired Entertainment launches its premium iGaming portfolio in South Africa
data.description:
Inspired Entertainment (INSE) announced the launch of its premium iGaming portfolio in South Africa. The company noted that it is starting off with six customers, including Hollywood Bets and Betway, which together account for a significant proportion of the online gaming market according to industry estimates. Notably, the South African market is forecast to double in size by 2030.

The launch in South Africa was enabled through Inspired Entertainment's (INSE) ongoing agreement with Light & Wonder (LNW) and distributed via the Light & Wonder iGaming content marketplace.

Inspired Entertainment said it continues to grow its global interactive footprint across regulated markets, with South Africa representing a key addition to its expanding international presence, a market that is predicted to double in size by 2030. The launch was said to reflect the company's ongoing focus on delivering innovative, engaging, and high-quality gaming experiences to players worldwide.

"Launching our iGaming portfolio with such well-known South African operators, collectively representing the majority of the market, marks an important milestone in our expansion. Providing access to approximately 90% of the market, we are excited to collaborate with these well-known leaders to deliver fresh, engaging experiences to players, including localized features tailored specifically to South African audiences. We look forward to demonstrating how our content performs in this dynamic market," highlighted CEO Brooks Pierce.

data.title: Eni, Repsol plan to start exporting Venezuelan gas by 2031 - Bloomberg
data.description:
Italy's Eni (E) and Spain's Repsol (REPYY) (REPYF) reached a deal with the Venezuelan government to start exporting natural gas from the country by the end of 2031, Bloomberg reported Monday.

The agreement allows the companies to more than double production at the massive Perla offshore field and export the fuel as liquefied natural gas from a floating terminal, according to the report.

The Perla field, which Eni (E) and Repsol (REPYY) (REPYF) discovered in 2009 and lies in shallow waters near Venezuela's border with Colombia, holds an estimated 17 Tcf of gas, making it one of the largest natural gas discoveries in Latin America.

The field currently produces 585M cf/day of gas, supplying power plants, petrochemical facilities, factories, and homes in western Venezuela; according to the report, the new agreement with the government allows the companies to start exporting once they are supplying 645M cf/day for domestic use.

Eni (E) and Repsol (REPYY) (REPYF) also received assurances that the companies will be compensated for billions of dollars worth of gas they have pumped for Venezuela at the field over the years but were not compensated, the report said.

The companies, which plan to install two more platforms in the field by 2028 and start exporting once they reach 1.2B cf/day of production, expect to submit a final development plan to state-owned PDVSA by June, according to the report.

data.title: Micron alone accounts for 51% of all S&P 500 EPS revisions since the war began: Goldman Sachs
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) alone is responsible for 51% of the entire S&P 500's EPS revision since the war began, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

MU's consensus EPS growth estimate for 2026 stands at a staggering 605%, with its EPS revision since February 27 coming in at 93%, nearly doubling in just weeks as the semiconductor demand surge tied to AI infrastructure and defense tech spending filtered through analyst models.

Goldman Sachs had already flagged Micron as a standout ahead of its earnings, raising its own 2026 EPS estimates 19% above consensus.

Outside of Micron and Broadcom (AVGO) in Info Tech, the remaining top contributors are almost entirely energy companies, reflecting how the conflict has upended oil and gas earnings expectations.

Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) together contributed 24% of the index's EPS revision, with revisions of 44% and 67% respectively since February 27.

Occidental Petroleum (OXY) posted the most dramatic revision of any energy name at 251%, albeit contributing just 2% to the index-level revision due to its smaller weight. ConocoPhillips (COP), Valero Energy (VLO), and EOG Resources (EOG) also featured prominently, each contributing 3–5% of the total S&P 500 revision.

data.title: Longleaf Partners Global Fund exits BIO, DIS among Q1 moves
data.description:
Longleaf Partners Global Fund said that it ended the first quarter of 2026 with a P/V in the mid-50s%.

The fund, in its quarterly commentary, said that FedEx (FDX) and Glanbia (GLAPY) were top contributors, while Canal+ (CNALF), Delivery Hero (DHERO), IDP Education (IDPUF), and Mattel (MAT) were top detractors.

During the first quarter, the fund did not initiate any new positions. However, it increased its stakes in Canal+, Delivery Hero, and IDP Education by adding to its investments.

On the other hand, the fund exited Bio-Rad Laboratories (BIO) and Louis Hachette Group (LSHGF) to focus on holdings that are better positioned both qualitatively and quantitatively.

According to its quarterly commentary, the fund also exited a small, short-term, and successful investment in Louisiana-Pacific (LPF) after housing market optimism early in the year pushed the share price close to its appraisal value.

The fund also sold The Walt Disney Company (DIS) earlier in the quarter, as it was one of its higher P/V and more economically sensitive holdings, but hopes to revisit the investment in the future.

Source: Fund letter

data.title: Microsoft's Q3 likely to 'take back the narrative' amid cautious sentiment: MS
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is set to report fiscal third-quarter results on April 29, and Morgan Stanley believes the tech giant may look to “take back the narrative,” as investors remain cautious for several reasons. 

“Microsoft exited F2Q26 with clear evidence of durable commercial demand, yet investor focus remains centered on supply constraints and GPU allocation weighing on Azure growth and increasing concerns around Copilot's competitive positioning versus AI Labs,” analyst Keith Weiss wrote in a note to clients. “Last quarter, Microsoft delivered mid-teens revenue growth at scale with expanding operating margins, while forward indicators such as 110% YoY RPO growth (to ~$625 billion) and 39% YoY cRPO growth point to continued strength across the commercial portfolio. Into F3Q26 results, Azure growth remains governed by capacity availability rather than demand. At the same time, Microsoft continues to invest aggressively in AI infrastructure, with capex skewed towards short-lived, near-term revenue generating assets (GPUs/CPUs).”

Weiss, who has an Overweight rating and a $650 price target on Microsoft, said that Azure growth in the 39% range would be “clearly seen” as a positive, notable given he believes it's achievable. “Further, an improving Copilot product can lead to improving sentiment and continued momentum on top of the new 15M paid Copilot seat KPI from last quarter, as Microsoft penetrates its broader installed base,” Weiss added.

Aside from Azure capacity, which Microsoft allocates both internally and externally, other items worth monitoring include gross margins, operating margins, and the level of how constrained Azure, among other factors, Weiss added.

data.title: Cadence edges up as EDA arrives at its 'ChatGPT moment': Needham
data.description:
Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) was up 2% during Monday trading as Needham determined that its new agentic AI product could greatly improve the productivity of electronic design automation and chip design.

"The ChatGPT moment for EDA may have finally arrived, and agentic AI tools may be a game changer for the semiconductor industry, as massive 'tool calls' enabled by agentic AI, should lead to significant EDA license growth," said Needham analysts Charles Shi and Denis Pyatchanin in an investor report.

Needham maintained its Buy rating and inched up its price target to $400 from $390.

"We have felt that there has been some distance between AI and EDA over the past three years," Shi noted. "The 'old AI' EDA products have largely failed to move needles for CDNS and SNPS. We feel that Agentic AI EDA is a different beast. 10x, 100x, and potentially 1,000x productivity improvement in chip design enabled by agentic AI should fundamentally change EDA economics for the better."

"The use of agentic AI to automate EDA workflow means that the design cost is likely less determined by engineer salaries but more determined by token cost going forward," Shi added. "Agentic AI is an equalizer and will remove labor as a bottleneck for chip design."

Last week, Cadence announced a new deal with Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) and expanded its previous offering with Nvidia (NVDA).
